paper_title: Pollen and Environmental Analysis in the Balearic Islands

methods.description: Process for collecting and analyzing pollen samples from moss cushions and surface sedimentary samples to study current pollen rain.

steps.description: Both moss cushions and surface sedimentary samples were collected to perform pollen analyses on the current pollen rain.
steps.searchable_summary: collect moss cushions and surface sedimentary samples for pollen analysis

steps.description: Moss samples were treated following the standard procedures in palynology, including KOH, 200-µm sieving and acetolysis.
steps.searchable_summary: treat moss samples with KOH, sieving, and acetolysis

steps.description: Surface sedimentary samples were analyzed using a 7-µm nylon handheld sieve and heavy-liquid separation.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sedimentary samples with sieving and heavy-liquid separation

steps.description: Pollen counts and identification were performed using a 400×, 600× and 1000× light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: count and identify pollen using light microscopy

steps.description: Pollen percentages are based on pollen sum, excluding Pinus due to its overrepresentation.
The taxonomic identification of pollen grains was based on pollen atlases, morphological keys, and the pollen reference collection.
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen taxa using atlases and reference collections

methods.method_name: Redundancy Analysis (RDA) of Pollen Taxa and Environmental Variables
methods.description: Ordination techniques to evaluate the relationship between modern pollen sampling sites, selected pollen taxa, and environmental variables.

steps.description: Detrended correspondence analysis (DCA) was performed to determine the most appropriate ordination techniques for these data.
steps.searchable_summary: perform DCA to select ordination techniques

steps.description: Variance inflation factors (VIF) were calculated for all environmental variables to assess redundancy.
Environmental variables with VIF values greater than 20.0 were considered highly collinear and removed.
The RDA of pollen, sites, and environmental variables was conducted using a combination forward/backward stepwise model selection.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct RDA with stepwise model selection

steps.description: ANOVA permutation tests were performed to identify the influence of each predictor on the structure of the pollen dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA permutation tests on RDA model

steps.description: Pearson's correlation coefficients (r) were calculated to evaluate the relationship between pollen taxa and each environmental variable.
